diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secondary polyuria is the expected consequence of primary over-drinking.
Polyuria occurs in osmotic diuresis but is non-specific; here it follows behavioural over-hydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excessive water intake (>3 L) to 'flush the sugar'

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fear-driven volitional over-drinking is the primary behavioural driver of the whole presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute health anxiety / fear of diabetic coma and insulin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The anxiety is the causal engine of the over-drinking and the presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of vomiting and abdominal pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting and abdominal pain are common in DKA; their absence lowers its probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved hydration and comfortable breathing (no Kussmaul respirations)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Kussmaul breathing signals acidosis; comfortable breathing argues strongly against significant ketoacidosis.
Ketotic breath accompanies significant ketosis; its absence supports the negative ketone result.

symptoms.symptom_name: Alert, oriented sensorium (no drowsiness or confusion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Altered mentation is characteristic of HHS and severe DKA; a fully alert patient argues strongly against them.

investigation_details.expected_result: 12.4 mmol/L — mildly elevated, far below the HHS range (typically >33 mmol/L)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without a glucose value the degree of hyperglycaemia cannot be gauged; in telehealth this must come from a home glucometer or prompt in-person testing.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Glucose is far below the hyperosmolar threshold, excluding HHS.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Anxiety-driven psychogenic polydipsia with mild hyperglycaemia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only mild elevation is consistent with a behavioural rather than emergency metabolic cause.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The ketone value is the single most decisive test to exclude DKA; the AI Doctor cannot perform it and must obtain a home ketone reading or route to testing.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative blood ketones with mild glucose in an alert patient excludes ketoacidosis at the time of testing.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Already known from prior work-up; confirms the autoimmune (LADA) basis and informs long-term progression to insulin.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: GAD antibody positivity in an adult with slowly-progressive diabetes defines LADA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stable vital signs (BP 130/82, HR 95, RR 18, SpO2 100%, afebrile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamic stability and normal oxygenation argue against significant metabolic decompensation; these are clinician-measured and sealed — the AI infers stability from patient-reportable features, not measured vitals.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, absent vomiting/abdominal pain, comfortable breathing, no ketotic breath, a fully alert sensorium and negative ketones safely exclude an acute hyperglycaemic emergency.
Fear-driven excessive water intake driving secondary polyuria identifies the true, benign cause once emergencies are excluded.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Capillary blood glucose (home glucometer or in-person point-of-care)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the degree of hyperglycaemia; mild elevation reassures, a markedly high reading escalates.
The decisive test to exclude DKA; must be obtained via home meter or prompt in-person testing.
Assesses medium-term glycaemic control and oral therapy efficacy at GP follow-up.
Characterises residual beta-cell function and confirms LADA to guide the timing of future insulin therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate emergency-department venous blood gas and electrolytes
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a stable, ketone-negative, alert patient with only mild hyperglycaemia; routing to ED for bloods here would be over-triage.
